provenance.corpus: hand-curated seed + model-authored expansion, frequency-informed (full FrequencyWords/OpenSubtitles pipeline pending)
provenance.authoringModel: claude-fable-5 (bands 5-10 + additions; human review incomplete: docs/pack-review-2026.10.md)

items.explanation: und means and — the most common German word.

examples.source: The dog wants to go outside.
A dog is waiting at the door.
examples.target: Ein Hund wartet an der Tür.

examples.source: The water in the lake is clear.
examples.target: Das Wasser im See ist klar.

examples.source: I am reading a good book.
The book is on the table.
examples.target: Das Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.

examples.source: We will see each other again.
We are driving to Berlin tomorrow.
The child is playing in the garden.
The year is going by quickly.

items.explanation: es gibt is the fixed phrase for 'there is / there are'.

examples.source: There is a lot to see.

items.explanation: zum Beispiel means for example (abbreviated z. B.).

examples.source: Animals, for example cats and dogs.
examples.target: Tiere, zum Beispiel Katzen und Hunde.

examples.source: We are driving with the car.
It is raining; we are going nevertheless.
It is late; we are staying nevertheless.
examples.target: Es ist spät, trotzdem bleiben wir.

examples.source: He is tired; he works nevertheless.
examples.target: Er ist müde, trotzdem arbeitet er.

items.explanation: deshalb means therefore / that is why.

examples.source: That is why I am here.
It is cold, therefore we are staying inside.
examples.target: Es ist kalt, deshalb bleiben wir drinnen.

examples.source: We are driving through the night.
He is waving with his hand.

items.explanation: natürlich as a sentence word means of course.

examples.source: Of course you can come along.
An old man is sitting there.

items.explanation: die Frau (feminine) — woman; plural Frauen (also Mrs/Ms).

examples.source: A woman is asking for directions.
examples.target: Eine Frau fragt nach dem Weg.

examples.source: He is saving money for a trip.
examples.target: Er spart Geld für eine Reise.

examples.source: The children are going to school.

items.explanation: ich glaube means I think / I believe.

examples.source: I think he is at home.
examples.target: Ich glaube, er ist zu Hause.

items.explanation: genau means exactly — also used alone as agreement.

examples.source: By the way, the film was great.
By the way, Anna is coming too.
The month begins on a Monday.

items.explanation: guten Morgen is the standard morning greeting.

examples.source: Good morning, the coffee is ready.
examples.target: Guten Morgen, der Kaffee ist fertig.

examples.source: Good morning, did you sleep well?
examples.target: Guten Morgen, hast du gut geschlafen?

items.explanation: bis bald means see you soon.

examples.source: The father is playing with the child.
examples.target: Der Vater spielt mit dem Kind.

examples.source: The family is travelling by train.
examples.target: Die Familie fährt mit dem Zug.

items.explanation: etwas means something — it never changes form.

examples.source: I would like to eat something.

items.explanation: nichts means nothing — it never changes form.
ich weiß nicht means I don't know.

examples.target: Ich weiß nicht, tut mir leid.

examples.source: I don't know if that works.
examples.target: Ich weiß nicht, ob das geht.

examples.source: I am staying at home today.
We are staying at home tonight.
examples.target: Wir bleiben heute Abend zu Hause.
Zu Hause ist es am schönsten.

examples.source: The keys are on the table.
examples.target: Die Schlüssel liegen auf dem Tisch.

items.explanation: das Zimmer (neuter) — room; the plural is also Zimmer.

examples.source: The room is warm and quiet.
examples.target: Das Zimmer ist warm und ruhig.

items.explanation: das Fenster (neuter) — window; the plural is also Fenster.

examples.source: The window looks onto the garden.
We are buying bread at the market.
examples.target: Wir kaufen Brot auf dem Markt.

examples.source: The milk is in the fridge.
An apple is lying in the bowl.
examples.target: Ein Apfel liegt in der Schale.

items.explanation: der Lehrer (masculine) — teacher; the plural is also Lehrer.

examples.source: The child is three years old.
examples.target: Das Kind ist drei Jahre alt.
Noch fünf Tage bis zur Reise.

examples.source: Excuse me, where is the station?
Excuse me, what time is it?
Excuse me, is this seat free?

items.explanation: ich möchte means I would like — the polite way to ask.

examples.source: I would like a table for two.
examples.target: Ich möchte einen Tisch für zwei.

items.explanation: guten Abend is the evening greeting.

examples.source: Good evening, the table is ready.
examples.target: Guten Abend, der Tisch ist bereit.

items.explanation: gute Nacht is said before sleep.
bis später means see you later.

examples.source: See you later at the park.
I have to go — see you later!

items.explanation: der Montag — Monday. Days of the week are masculine.

examples.source: On Thursday the market is open.
examples.target: Am Donnerstag ist der Markt offen.

examples.source: On Saturday we go to the market.
examples.target: Am Samstag gehen wir zum Markt.

examples.source: We are waiting one more hour.
A thousand lights in the city.
The café is between the bank and the park.
examples.target: Das Café liegt zwischen Bank und Park.

examples.source: Between twelve and one we eat.
examples.target: Zwischen zwölf und eins essen wir.

examples.source: It costs less than ten euros.
examples.target: Es kostet weniger als zehn Euro.

items.explanation: wie geht's is the casual how are you.

examples.source: We are solving the problem together.
A quiet life in the country.
examples.target: Ein ruhiges Leben auf dem Land.

examples.source: At the end everything turned out fine.
At the beginning of the year.

items.explanation: das Bild (neuter) — picture / image; plural Bilder.
der Film (masculine) — film / movie; plural Filme.

examples.source: We are watching a film tonight.
examples.target: Wir sehen heute Abend einen Film.

items.explanation: die Nachricht (feminine) — message; plural Nachrichten (also 'the news').

examples.source: The telephone will not stop ringing.

items.explanation: der Computer (masculine) — computer; the plural is also Computer.
sowieso means anyway / in any case.

examples.source: That is no problem at all.
Too much sugar in the tea.
There is no reason to worry.
examples.target: Es gibt keinen Grund zur Sorge.

examples.source: A good example helps a lot.

items.explanation: die Geschichte (feminine) — story, and also history; plural Geschichten.
die Reise (feminine) — journey / trip; plural Reisen.
das Ziel (neuter) — goal / destination; plural Ziele.

examples.source: We are almost at the goal.
Sport is good for the heart.

items.explanation: der Januar — January. Months are masculine.

examples.target: Der Februar ist der kürzeste Monat.
Der April bringt Regen und Sonne.

examples.source: In June the days are long.
examples.target: Im Juni sind die Tage lang.

examples.source: In July we swim in the lake.
examples.target: Im Juli schwimmen wir im See.
Der Juli ist warm und hell.
Der September fühlt sich neu an.

examples.source: In November it gets dark early.
examples.target: Im November wird es früh dunkel.
Der November ist still und grau.

items.explanation: übermorgen means the day after tomorrow — one word in German.

examples.source: See you the day after tomorrow.
The day after tomorrow is a holiday.
We are meeting the day after tomorrow.

items.explanation: auf jeden Fall means in any case / definitely.

examples.source: In any case, I am coming.
In any case, take an umbrella.
examples.target: Nimm auf jeden Fall einen Schirm.
Zum Glück waren wir früh dran.

items.explanation: der Bahnhof (masculine) — train station; plural Bahnhöfe.

examples.source: The train station is old but beautiful.
examples.target: Der Bahnhof ist alt, aber schön.

examples.source: We are meeting at the train station.
The bus drives to the airport.
The hotel is by the sea.
A small hotel in the old town.
examples.target: Ein kleines Hotel in der Altstadt.

examples.source: We are eating at the restaurant today.
The market smells of fresh bread.
examples.target: Der Markt riecht nach frischem Brot.

examples.source: The church stands in the centre.
An old church made of stone.

items.explanation: das Krankenhaus (neuter) — hospital ('sick house'); plural Krankenhäuser.

examples.target: Das Krankenhaus ist in der Nähe.

examples.source: I am studying at the library.
The children are playing in the garden.
We are walking through the park.
The park is old and quiet.
examples.target: Der Park ist alt und still.

examples.source: What will the weather be tomorrow?
We are sitting in the sun.
A warm wind from the south.
examples.target: Ein warmer Wind aus dem Süden.

items.explanation: vorgestern means the day before yesterday — one word in German.

examples.source: That was the day before yesterday.
The letter came the day before yesterday.
The day before yesterday it snowed.
The mountain is covered in snow.
The river flows to the sea.
The lake glitters in the sun.
examples.target: Der See glitzert in der Sonne.

examples.source: We are swimming in the lake.
We are hiking through the forest.
A bird sits in the tree.
A flower stands in the vase.
examples.target: Eine Blume steht in der Vase.

examples.source: The cat sleeps on the chair.
examples.target: Die Katze schläft auf dem Stuhl.

examples.source: A horse stands in the field.
examples.target: Ein Pferd steht auf der Wiese.

examples.source: The dog has a fine nose.
examples.target: Der Hund hat eine feine Nase.

examples.source: I have an appointment at the doctor's.
examples.target: Ich habe einen Termin beim Arzt.
